sourceMovie.title: Fury
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Fury works as a war movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The war and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Greyhound
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Fury clicked for you, Greyhound (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in war and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Fury?
faq.answer: Try Greyhound, The Longest Day, Cross of Iron—each shares DNA with Fury while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Fury?
faq.answer: Fury (2014) is best described as War, Drama, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Fury worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this war lane.
